display.purpose: Page heading only — the word 'Sudoku'. Wide letter-spacing creates presence without size inflation.

body.purpose: All controls, button labels, messages, difficulty selector. The workhorse size.

label.purpose: Number pad buttons, inline loading text. Same size as body; context distinguishes role.

motion.name: cell-transition
motion.purpose: Cell state changes (selection, hover). Fastest transition — cells change frequently.

motion.name: spinner
motion.purpose: Loading spinner rotation during async operations (hint, solve, check).

breakpoints.name: mobile
breakpoints.purpose: Below this: reduced padding, smaller heading (1.6rem), smaller button font (0.9rem). The --cell variable handles board scaling automatically.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: Used for initiating actions: New Game, Check Puzzle. One per control group.
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { background: #1565c0; color: #fff; border: 2px solid #1565c0; border-radius: 4px; padding: 0.5rem 1.25rem; font-family: Georgia, serif; font-size: 1rem; cursor: pointer; transition: background-color 0.12s, border-color 0.12s, color 0.12s; } .ds-btn-primary:hover { background: #0d47a1; border-color: #0d47a1; } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #1565c0; outline-offset: 2px; } .ds-btn-primary:disabled { opacity: 0.38; cursor: not-allowed; }

components.name: Default Button
components.description: Secondary actions: Hint, Solve, Create Puzzle, Back to Play, Clear Grid.
components.css: .ds-btn { background: #fff; color: #111; border: 2px solid #333; border-radius: 4px; padding: 0.5rem 1.25rem; font-family: Georgia, serif; font-size: 1rem; cursor: pointer; transition: background-color 0.12s, color 0.12s; } .ds-btn:hover:not(:disabled) { background: #333; color: #fff; } .ds-btn:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #333; outline-offset: 2px; } .ds-btn:disabled { opacity: 0.38; cursor: not-allowed; }

components.name: Cell — User Filled
components.description: A cell the user has entered a digit into. Blue accent marks the user's work.
components.css: .ds-cell { width: 52px; height: 52px; border: 1px solid #bbb; display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; font-family: Georgia, serif; font-size: 25px; background: #fff; color: #111; cursor: pointer; user-select: none; transition: background-color 0.08s; } .ds-cell--user-filled { color: #1565c0; font-weight: 400; } .ds-cell--given { background: #e2e2e2; font-weight: 700; color: #111; cursor: default; } .ds-cell--selected { background: #90caf9 !important; } .ds-cell--same { background: #dbeeff; } .ds-cell--error { background: #f8d7da !important; color: #c62828 !important; font-weight: 700; }

components.name: Cell — Given Clue
components.description: A locked clue cell provided with the puzzle. Heavier weight + gray background marks immutability — two independent signals.
components.css: .ds-cell-given-standalone { width: 52px; height: 52px; border: 1px solid #bbb; display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; font-family: Georgia, serif; font-size: 25px; background: #e2e2e2; color: #111; font-weight: 700; cursor: default; user-select: none; }

components.name: Cell — Error State
components.description: A cell containing a digit that conflicts with another in its row, column, or box. Color AND text weight both change — color is never the sole error signal.
components.css: .ds-cell-error-standalone { width: 52px; height: 52px; border: 1px solid #bbb; display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; font-family: Georgia, serif; font-size: 25px; background: #f8d7da; color: #c62828; font-weight: 700; cursor: pointer; user-select: none; }

components.name: Number Pad Button
components.description: One of ten input buttons (1–9 plus Clear) below the board. Scales with the --cell variable.
components.css: .ds-num-btn { width: 52px; height: 44px; font-family: Georgia, serif; font-size: 22px; border: 2px solid #333; border-radius: 4px; background: #fff; color: #111; cursor: pointer; transition: background-color 0.1s, color 0.1s; display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; } .ds-num-btn:hover:not(:disabled) { background: #333; color: #fff; } .ds-num-btn:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #333; outline-offset: 2px; } .ds-num-btn:disabled { opacity: 0.38; cursor: not-allowed; }

components.name: Message — Warning
components.description: Informational status message: hints used, puzzle loading, ambiguous puzzle states.
components.html: Puzzle not yet solved — keep going.
components.css: .ds-message { font-family: Georgia, serif; font-size: 1rem; padding: 0.5rem 1.25rem; border-radius: 4px; } .ds-message--warn { background: #fff3cd; border: 1px solid #ffc107; color: #7a5700; } .ds-message--success { background: #d4edda; border: 1px solid #c3e6cb; color: #155724; font-size: 1.2rem; font-weight: 700; } .ds-message--error { background: #f8d7da; border: 1px solid #f5c6cb; color: #721c24; }

components.name: Message — Success
components.description: Puzzle completion feedback. Larger and bolder than other messages — the completion moment earns emphasis.
components.css: .ds-message-success-standalone { font-family: Georgia, serif; font-size: 1.2rem; font-weight: 700; padding: 0.5rem 1.25rem; border-radius: 4px; background: #d4edda; border: 1px solid #c3e6cb; color: #155724; }

narrative.overview:
The Ruled Grid is a design vocabulary derived from the logic of the puzzle itself. The 9×9 board is not dressed up — it is the interface. Every design decision defers to a single question: does this serve the act of solving? The aesthetic is that of technical drawing — precise ink on a neutral field, not warm, not decorative, not themed.

This system rejects the overcrowded game-UI aesthetic — badges, streaks, score counters, pop-up overlays, dark-pattern mechanics — as categorically wrong for what this product is. It equally rejects the generic SaaS template: rounded card grids, gradient buttons, and the reflexive institutional blue that signals 'used a UI kit.' The grid already has strong enough visual logic that the interface needs only to clarify it, not decorate it.

As a portfolio project, the design proves technical judgment through restraint. The signal is in the states — selected, given, error, success — each distinct and deliberate, each communicating without ornamentation.
keyCharacteristics: Single-page tool with zero navigation chrome; the board is the whole screen
Cell states are the primary visual vocabulary — six distinct states, each communicating something specific about the puzzle
One accent color, used only where the user acts or initiates action
Completely flat; depth conveyed by tonal contrast and border weight alone
One font family carries every typographic role: heading, controls, messages, grid digits

rules.name: The One Accent Rule
rules.body: #1565c0 appears in exactly two roles: user-filled digit color, and primary button background. It marks where the user is and what the user can do. Apply it to a decorative element and the semantic contract breaks.

rules.name: The Two-Signal Error Rule
rules.body: Error states always change both background color AND text/border color. Color alone is not a sufficient indicator (WCAG 1.4.1). The cell error state (#f8d7da bg + #c62828 text) and the error message (#f8d7da bg + #721c24 text) both enforce this.

rules.name: The Flat-By-Default Rule
rules.body: Surfaces never lift. box-shadow is prohibited at all scales. The grid's depth comes from the structural weight of its ink, not from simulated light sources. If you feel the urge to add a shadow, add tonal contrast or border weight instead.

rules.name: The One Family Rule
rules.body: No secondary font is introduced. Georgia carries the heading, the controls, the messages, and the grid digits. Mixing sans-serif controls into a serif frame breaks the One-Voice system.

rules.name: The Weight Contrast Rule
rules.body: Given clues (700 weight) and user-filled digits (400 weight) differ in weight AND color. Never communicate immutability through color alone.

dos: Do reserve #1565c0 (Steel Authority) for user-filled digit color and primary action buttons only
Do use 3px solid #111111 for board and box boundaries, 1px #bbbbbb for cell boundaries — this contrast is structural
Do derive any grid-adjacent sizing from the --cell custom property
Do mark errors with both background color AND text color change (WCAG 1.4.1)
Do maintain the weight distinction between given cells (700) and user-filled cells (400)
Do keep focus indicators visible: 2px solid accent at 2px offset for primary, 2px solid #333333 for default
donts: Don't add box-shadow to any surface — the system is completely flat
Don't add a second typeface — Georgia is the only family
Don't use border-left or border-right greater than 1px as a colored accent on any container
Don't apply #1565c0 to inactive states, decorative elements, or icons
Don't round the board's corners — border-radius: 0 on .board is intentional
Don't introduce the mobile-game aesthetic: no badges, streaks, achievements, or reward animations
Don't use gradient text, glassmorphism, or decorative blur effects
Don't use identical card grids, gradient buttons, or side-stripe bordered callouts
Don't merge or omit cell states — each state communicates specific puzzle information
